Empires & Puzzles Phantom of the Opera Review: The Masked Maestro of Mana Control

He doesn't just sing; he silences. Discover why Phantom of the Opera's unique 'Ballad of Obsession' makes him a tactical nightmare for enemy buffers, even if his stats have seen better days. Is he the control freak your roster needs?
Phantom of the Opera: The Masked Maestro of Mana Control

Phantom of the Opera arrived in June 2023 as the star of the "Secrets of the Opera" event, bringing with him a mechanic so unique it confused half the player base and frustrated the other half. He is a

In his prime, he was a stat monster. Today, in the era of 2026 power creep, he's more of a tactical specialist—a "Reverse Gazelle" who forces his enemies to dance to a tune of despair. He doesn't just hit them; he locks them down, preventing them from gaining any new buffs while starving them of mana and healing. He’s the guy you bring when you want to stop the enemy from having any fun.

The Phantom is depicted in his iconic masked glory, striking a pose that screams "drama queen." The art style fits the operatic theme perfectly, with a dark, mysterious purple palette that contrasts sharply with the bright stage lights implied in the background. It’s theatrical, elegant, and slightly menacing—exactly what you’d expect from a guy who lives in a basement and obsesses over sopranos. Compared to the more abstract "Construct" or "Astral" designs of late 2025, the Phantom feels grounded in a classic, gothic aesthetic.
Let's be real: Time has not been kind to the Phantom's base stats.
His 945 Attack, once considered "hitting like a truck," is now dwarfed by modern attackers sitting at 1300+.
With 933 Defense and 1707 HP, he is squishy by modern standards. He
needs Limit Breaks (LB1 and LB2) and full emblems to survive a single hit from a modern sniper like
However, his value isn't in his raw numbers anymore—it's in his ability to completely shut down enemy momentum.
"Ballad of Obsession" is a fascinating, double-edged sword. It hits three targets (Target and Nearby) and forces them to "dance."
clears all status effects from her allies and prevents new ones, the Phantom clears all effects from his
and prevents them from getting new ones.
The -40% Mana Generation and -40% Healing is a brutal combination. It effectively pauses the enemy's game plan.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Opera family bonus provides raw stats (Attack/Defense/HP) for fielding unique family members. While stat boosts are always nice, the Opera family is relatively small, making full synergy harder to pull off than larger families like
He heals 10% HP whenever an ailment on him expires or is cleansed. This is a fantastic survival tool, especially against DoT-heavy teams. It often triggers right when you need it most.
He is immune to Attack Down. This ensures his 405% hit always lands with full force, which is critical since his base stats are already lagging.
A simple 20% boost at the start of battle. It helps him keep up with the power creep slightly, ensuring his opening salvo still stings.
Upon release, Phantom was considered "OP" and "Broken" due to his high damage and unique lockout mechanic. He was a staple in defenses.
The damage that was once "insane" is now merely "decent." The 900-stat era is long gone. Modern heroes have 30-40% higher base stats.
in Rush Wars/Tournaments where his Average speed becomes Very Fast, allowing him to lock down enemies before they can fire.

His average speed becomes a non-issue, and his lockout is devastating when fired early.
He is the ultimate anti-meta pick here, stripping buffs and preventing new ones.

If you pulled him, rejoice! Even with older stats, his mechanic is unique and powerful. He will carry you through map stages and raids by simply shutting down bosses. Prioritize his survival with emblems.
He is a luxury tool. Don't strip your LB2
for him, but if you have spare mats, he solves specific puzzle boards (like heavy taunt/dodge teams). Do not use him on Titans—blocking your own Defense Down is a DPS loss.
"Wtf is happening here? Insane base stat buffs... but he blocks my own ailments?!"
In mid-2023, players screamed about his 955 power being "game-breaking." Reading those comments in 2026 is almost funny, as we now look at 3-star heroes creeping up on that number.
said many players, realizing that while annoying, the Phantom didn't protect his team like the top-tier tanks of the time.
Phantom of the Opera remains one of the most mechanically interesting heroes in Empires & Puzzles. He demands you play differently—thinking about what you

do as much as what you can. While his stats have gathered dust, his song still has the power to silence the most modern, overpowered compositions if timed correctly. Just remember: when the Phantom sings,
At the time of this review, the Phantom does not have a costume. If one is released, expect it to drastically update his stats to modern standards, likely making him a terror once again.
The opera isn't over until the fat lady sings... or until the Phantom makes her dance.

Is Phantom of the Opera still worth the tabards in 2026? We analyze his unique 'Ballad' mechanic, compare his aging stats to modern heroes, and explain why he's the ultimate 'Reverse Gazelle' for strategic players.
